title: Mildly Spicy Norwegian Fish Pasta
description: A spicy and crumbly Norwegian dish featuring linguine pasta and pescatarian-friendly ingredients, perfect for a hangover cure. This recipe combines the flavors of Norway with a kick of heat.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the lamination puff to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
Cut the puff into small squares and bake for 15 minutes or until golden brown.
Cook the linguine pasta according to package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the cod fillets and cook until opaque and flaky.
Add the diced tomatoes, norwegian chili flakes, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Combine the cooked pasta and skillet mixture, then top with crumbled lamination puff.
